news.summary: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse has expressed confidence that XRP could capture a substantial portion of the cross-border payments market, aiming to handle around 14% of the transactions currently processed through SWIFT within the next five years. During a presentation at the XRP APEX 2025 event in Singapore, Garlinghouse highlighted XRP's role in enhancing global liquidity management, rather than solely replacing existing messaging systems. He elaborated on SWIFT's current dual functions of messaging and liquidity management, indicating that banks currently oversee the liquidity aspect. Garlinghouse's insights were reported by Coindesk. For the complete article, please visit a financial news platform.
news.headline: Ripple CEO predicts XRP could handle 14% of SWIFT's cross-border payment volume in 5 years by streamlining global liquidity.

news.summary: A report on the vertical farming market in the US indicates that in 2024, it was valued at USD 1.11 billion, with a projected growth to USD 2.55 billion by 2029, showcasing a CAGR of 18.2% during the forecast period. Factors contributing to this growth include decreasing arable land, increased consumer demand for local pesticide-free produce, and advancements in hydroponic, aeroponic, and aquaponic technologies. The market is also benefiting from investments in controlled environment agriculture, automation, and AI in farming solutions. Additionally, rising demand for climate-resilient farming systems and government support for sustainable and urban farming projects are further propelling market expansion. The key players in the industry include Signify Holding, Freight Farms, AeroFarms, Sky Greens, Spread, Plenty, Valoya, Everlight Electronics, and Heliospectra AB. Building-based vertical farms are likely to dominate the market due to their ability to operate in various environments without constraints, particularly in areas with limited fertile land availability.
news.headline: US vertical farming market valued at USD 1.11 billion in 2024; projected to reach USD 2.55 billion by 2029, with 18.2% CAGR.

news.summary: RLJ Lodging Trust, a company listed on NYSE under the ticker symbol RLJ, headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland, declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per common share on June 30, 2025. news.summary: AB "Rokiškio sūris" has injected EUR 10 million into UAB "Ateities ūkis" by purchasing 29.67% of its authorized capital to bolster raw milk production in Lithuania. UAB "Ateities ūkis" is under the Agrokoncernas group, focusing on raw milk production. AB Rokiškio sūris views this move as advantageous, foreseeing a rise in top-notch raw milk output. The management assures that the transaction is financially sound and will not drastically affect the company's overall performance and financial standing.
news.headline: AB "Rokiškio sūris" invests EUR 10 million in UAB "Ateities ūkis", acquiring 29.67% stake to boost raw milk production.

news.headline: CaringKind raises $1.5M at 29th Forget-Me-Not Gala for Alzheimer's and dementia care. 600+ guests attended.

news.summary: Last week, a report was released stating that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 7.3%, with a total of 11.1 million people currently unemployed. This is an increase from the previous month and marks a concerning trend in the job market. The report also highlighted that the number of job losses in the retail and hospitality sectors have been particularly high. The government is looking into implementing measures to address this issue and support those who have lost their jobs. Additionally, there has been a slight decrease in the GDP growth rate, which now stands at 3.5%. Experts predict that further interventions may be needed to stabilize the economy and mitigate the impact of these developments.
news.headline: NFI completes $600M offering of second lien notes.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) experienced a decline amidst escalating geopolitical tensions following Israel's attack on Iran. Traders are questioning whether this signifies a complete crash or simply a temporary reaction. In a recent post on June 13, crypto trader Jackis humorously referred to Bitcoin's drop as a journey to "Goblin Town," speculating on whether markets are overreacting to fear-inducing headlines. Jackis highlighted that despite the alarming situation, previous conflicts in the Middle East have led to emotional yet brief market downturns. This instance may also be driven by fear-induced selling rather than a fundamental breakdown. Jackis underscored that Bitcoin maintains a bullish trajectory, currently consolidating within a vertical ascending pattern. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin slipped after Israel's attack on Iran, causing traders to question whether it's a crash or just an overreaction.

news.summary: Lennar Corporation is set to announce its second-quarter earnings on June 16th, following the market close. Analysts predict earnings of $1.94 per share, a decrease from $3.38 per share in the previous year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $8.19 billion, down from $8.77 billion last year. The company recently approved a 50 cents per share dividend for both Class A and Class B common stock. Lennar's stock price closed at $112.15, up 0.6% on Thursday. For more details, including analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page.
news.headline: Lennar (NYSE: LEN) to announce Q2 earnings on June 16, expected at $1.94 EPS and $8.19B revenue. Stock closed at $112.15.

news.summary: Perma-Pipe International Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PPIH) released its financial results for the first quarter ending April 30, 2025. The company reported sales of $46.7 million for the quarter, marking a $12.4 million, or 36.2%, increase from the previous year's $34.3 million. Net income attributable to common stock also saw significant growth, reaching $5.0 million, which represents a $3.6 million, or 243%, surge from the $1.4 million reported in the corresponding quarter of the prior year. The company's President highlighted these positive financial outcomes.
news.headline: Perma-Pipe Int'l Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: PPIH) Q1 fiscal 2025: $46.7M sales, $5.0M net income (243% increase).

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, Multi Ways Holdings Limited, a prominent provider of heavy construction equipment for sales and rental in Singapore and nearby areas, disclosed the filing of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 20-F to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. This report includes the Company's financial outcomes for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024. Investors can view the detailed 2024 Annual Report, encompassing financial data, on the official investor relations website at www.multiwaysholdings.com. Multi Ways Holdings Limited is listed on NYSE American under the ticker symbol MWG.
news.headline: Multi Ways Holdings files Form 20-F for FY 2024 with financial results, accessible on their website.

news.summary: The latest release of Quantum Sim, version 5.0, introduces the secure GenAI middleware certified by HITRUST r2. This middleware has demonstrated over two years of successful production resilience. The new software powers QS Benefits and has led to a notable 40% decrease in call center times. It also facilitates the streamlining of provider contract management and prior authorization processes. Moreover, it enables rapid and secure development and deployment of AI solutions.
news.headline: "Quantum Gears releasing secure Quantum Sim 5.0 at AHIP 2025. HITRUST r2-certified GenAI middleware reduces call center times by 40%."
